name: Retro Arcade
description: Premium retro product-launch energy. Saturated cabinet plastics, boxed-toy packaging, pixel marquee typography, LED scoreboards, hard-edged controller buttons, campaign-safe full-bleed staging, and Kickstarter-style funnel moments that feel physical, collectible, and conversion-ready.
personality: physical product launch cabinet + boxed toy set energy + hard rectangular controller buttons with generous x-padding + pixel marquee type + breathable LED scoreboard counters + product packaging hero + no pill CTAs + no soft shadows + vivid campaign color

decorators.arcade-canvas: Full-page cabinet backdrop with deep indigo base, subtle scanline texture, and generous safe-area gutters for launch content.
decorators.arcade-marquee: High-contrast headline band with 4px border, alternating accent ticks, pixel display type, and tight uppercase rhythm.
decorators.arcade-bezel: Screen-like frame with thick dark border, inset cyan edge, and flat screen surface for product demos or campaign modules.
decorators.arcade-cabinet-shell: Physical cabinet wrapper for first-viewport product scenes, checkout kiosks, continue screens, and major funnel moments.
decorators.arcade-product-box: Boxed product or cartridge visual treatment with hard outline, diagonal art fields, spine labels, inventory marks, and physical launch proof.
decorators.arcade-button: Rectangular controller-style pressable control with 3px border, hard 6px offset shadow, saturated fill, active press translation, and generous horizontal padding so label/icon content never touches the walls.
decorators.arcade-controller-button: Square or rectangular hardware button grammar for CTAs, mode tabs, icon controls, and reward selection. Avoid pill silhouettes and cramped label/icon spacing.
decorators.arcade-cartridge: Reward tier shell shaped like a product cartridge or boxed founder pack, with top notch, status strip, price, contents, and active snap state.
decorators.arcade-ticket-rail: Horizontal proof/ticker rail for backer activity, press mentions, referral progress, and campaign momentum.
decorators.arcade-token: Hard-edged token badge with double border, notch-like details, uppercase label, and mono numeric counter support.
decorators.arcade-led-counter: Segment-display metric treatment with mono tabular numbers, dark strip background, electric cyan/yellow digits, and enough internal padding for numbers to breathe.
decorators.arcade-scoreboard: Large scoreboard module for funding progress, countdowns, launch KPIs, and proof counters. Counters should be grouped as display modules with clear gaps and inset breathing room.
decorators.arcade-pixel-border: Hard stepped border treatment using square corners, 2px inner highlight, and no blur.
decorators.arcade-progress-track: Thick campaign progress bar with segmented tick marks, hard border, and high-contrast fill states.
decorators.arcade-manual-panel: Instruction-manual surface with off-white fill, heavy border, numbered labels, and sticker-like accent tags.
decorators.arcade-sticker: Rotated small label with saturated fill, hard border, and playful campaign note styling.

spatial.surface_override: Use full-width bands, cabinet shells, boxed product scenes, cartridge racks, scoreboards, rails, counters, and instruction panels instead of generic card grids.

arcade-canvas.description: Full-page cabinet backdrop with deep indigo base, subtle scanline texture, and generous safe-area gutters for launch content.
arcade-canvas.intent: Use on page roots and full-bleed sections. The canvas should feel immersive while preserving a centered campaign safe area.

arcade-marquee.description: High-contrast headline band with 4px border, alternating accent ticks, pixel display type, and tight uppercase rhythm.
arcade-marquee.intent: Use for hero headlines, section titles, and primary campaign identity. The marquee must be a dominant visual signal.

arcade-bezel.description: Screen-like frame with thick dark border, inset cyan edge, and flat screen surface for product demos or campaign modules.
arcade-bezel.intent: Use for product demos, progress consoles, and pledge modules. The bezel replaces generic cards.

arcade-cabinet-shell.description: Physical cabinet wrapper for first-viewport product scenes, checkout kiosks, continue screens, and major funnel moments.
arcade-cabinet-shell.intent: Use when a section should feel like a physical launch object rather than a web card. It is ideal for the hero product stage, conversion kiosk, and final continue screen.

arcade-product-box.description: Boxed product or cartridge visual treatment with hard outline, diagonal art fields, spine labels, inventory marks, and physical launch proof.
arcade-product-box.intent: Use above the fold to make the product unmistakable. This should read like packaging, a creator kit, a hardware deck, a cartridge, or a collectible product box.

arcade-button.description: Rectangular controller-style pressable control with 3px border, hard 6px offset shadow, saturated fill, and active press translation.
arcade-button.intent: Use for all primary and secondary actions. Buttons should feel like raised hardware keys that depress into the cabinet surface. Avoid pill shapes for Retro Arcade CTAs.

arcade-controller-button.description: Square or rectangular hardware button grammar for CTAs, icon controls, mode tabs, and reward selection.
arcade-controller-button.intent: Use when a button should feel like an 80s controller or cabinet input. Press states should translate and collapse the hard shadow.

arcade-cartridge.description: Reward tier shell shaped like a product cartridge or boxed founder pack.
arcade-cartridge.intent: Use instead of pricing cards. Each tier should include a notch, label, price, inventory strip, contents, and a clearly active snap state.

arcade-token.description: Hard-edged token badge with double border, notch-like details, uppercase label, and mono numeric counter support.
arcade-token.intent: Use for compact status, backer levels, limited-run labels, and pledge tokens.

arcade-scoreboard.description: Large scoreboard module for funding progress, countdowns, launch KPIs, and proof counters.
arcade-scoreboard.intent: Use when metrics should feel public, live, and trustworthy. Pair counters with labels and progress text so the data stays accessible. Leave visible breathing room between the outer bezel, status label, counter modules, and progress track.

arcade-ticket-rail.description: Horizontal proof/ticker rail for backer activity, press mentions, referral progress, and campaign momentum.
arcade-ticket-rail.intent: Use as a full-width band, not a card. Keep ticker copy short and duplicate content for continuous marquee motion with reduced-motion fallback.

arcade-led-counter.description: Segment-display metric treatment with mono tabular numbers, dark strip background, and electric cyan/yellow digits.
arcade-led-counter.intent: Use for funding totals, countdowns, backer counts, and reward inventory. Numbers must be highly legible and never pressed against the border.

arcade-pixel-border.description: Hard stepped border treatment using square corners, 2px inner highlight, and no blur.
arcade-pixel-border.intent: Use when a section needs retro structure without becoming another card.

arcade-progress-track.description: Thick campaign progress bar with segmented tick marks, hard border, and high-contrast fill states.
arcade-progress-track.intent: Use for funding, stretch goals, tier availability, and onboarding completion. Progress should feel like campaign momentum.

arcade-manual-panel.description: Instruction-manual surface with off-white fill, heavy border, numbered labels, and sticker-like accent tags.
arcade-manual-panel.intent: Use for explanatory sections, product specs, story steps, and launch instructions. This replaces generic marketing cards.

arcade-sticker.description: Rotated small label with saturated fill, hard border, and playful campaign note styling.
arcade-sticker.intent: Use sparingly for limited-run notes, shipping states, early-bird labels, and proof cues.
